original_image_tags: A vertical, off-white paper document page, appearing aged with discoloration and foxing. It is the back side of a typed memo, showing the text from the front side in reverse (bleed-through). The page has two punched holes at the top edge. At the bottom, there are several official ink stamps, some oriented upside-down relative to the main document.

elements.description: Bottom left: inverted stamped text including 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', 'F B I', 'REC'D. ESPIONAGE', 'SEP 15 10 11 AM '35'. Bottom center: inverted stamped text 'REC'D. BELMONT', 'F.B.I.', 'Sep 15 12 20 PM '35'. Bottom right: faint inverted date stamp 'SEP 18 1935'.

judge.reason: Description A provides a general overview of stamps at the bottom, while Description B provides specific quoted text from those stamps, adding detail without contradiction.
